title: Blocks, CAPTCHAs, Rate Signals, Identity, and No-Evasion Boundary · Contract Workshop
summary: A public-interest site-quality team incrementally builds block detection and owner-escalation policy from original fixed response and HTML fixtures while retaining earlier authorization, HTTP, URL, provenance, politeness, accessibility, testing, security, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of block detection and owner-escalation policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pract…
objectives: Detect denial, challenge, login, consent, interstitial, throttling, suspicious redirects, and semantic block pages from multiple signals.
Treat CAPTCHA and human-verification challenges as stop and owner-coordination signals rather than puzzles to automate around.
Use an honest stable identity and refuse proxy rotation, fingerprint spoofing, account cycling, stealth plugins, or other defense evasion.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Block Detection
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop site-quality team handles case 6023 during a local-server failure rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to detect blocks and challenges, stop without evasion, preserve evidence, and coordinate with the target owner; the target owner must be able to pause the crawl; then change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-block-false-positive-review-1k2mju
# Competency: scraper-block-false-positive-review.
# Case 1505: a local-server failure rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: personal data must remain minimized and redacted.
# Changed case: change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance.
def evidence_scraper_block_false_positive_review_1k2mju(responses: int = 30, normal_pages: int = 24, blocked_pages: int = 6, evasion_attempts: int = 0, owner_escalations: int = 6) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1k2mju = "1k2mju-ujm2k1-1505"
    assert evidence_variant_1k2mju
    evidence_axes_1k2mju = (
        "authorization1k2mju",
        "url1505",
        "responseujm2k1",
        "tree1k2mju1505",
        "frontier15051k2mju",
        "reportujm2k11505",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1k2mju)) == 6
    if responses < 1 or normal_pages < 0 or blocked_pages < 0:
        return False, responses, "block-count"
    if normal_pages + blocked_pages != responses:
        return False, blocked_pages, "block-reconciliation"
    if evasion_attempts > 0:
        return False, evasion_attempts, "evasion-boundary"
    if owner_escalations != blocked_pages:
        return False, owner_escalations, "owner-coordination"
    assert normal_pages + blocked_pages == responses and evasion_attempts == 0
    return True, blocked_pages, "no-evasion-block-policy"
